goal: Add a contract-only Bluepilot enforcement surface that requires Maya-Core gate evidence before provider calls, write actions, or runtime execution can be reviewed for activation.
scope_out: Do not call Maya-Core from the new preflight surface, call providers, execute runtime, write files, write DB, issue permits, merge, deploy, or change package/workflow files.
claims: Bluepilot can describe the Maya-Core gate evidence required before provider, write, and runtime activation review.
Bluepilot can dry-validate submitted gate evidence without calling Maya-Core.
Missing or unreachable gate evidence blocks activation review and leaves all side effects false.
assumptions: Actual live Maya-Core reachability remains owned by the existing /health/maya-gate probe.
Actual provider, write, and runtime activation require later explicit locks.
dod: GET /probe/maya-core-gate-enforcement returns the contract-only enforcement boundary.
POST /probe/maya-core-gate-enforcement-preflight validates provider/write/runtime evidence without execution.
The eight-point readiness and operator dashboard mark Maya-Core Gate Enforcement as wired_contract_only.
Focused tests, typecheck, diff-check, and full builder tests pass.
stop_conditions: A live Maya-Core call from the new preflight surface, provider call, runtime execution, DB write, file write, permit issue, merge, deploy, package change, or workflow change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: The GOAT contract bundle made the first locked point wired_contract_only. Maya-Core Gate Enforcement remained represented only by /health/maya-gate and needed a dry activation evidence contract.
